methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Field AI, a Series A artificial intelligence and software company founded by Ali Agha and Shayegan Omidshafiei, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.0B against $405M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of nine same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, including five named comparables: Liquid AI ($2.4B, Series A), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), World Labs ($5.0B, Series B), Thinking Machines Lab ($12.0B, Seed), and Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B, Series B). The peer set spans one public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private entities.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, implies a $4.0B valuation. Peer-median benchmarking across the nine-company sample yields $2.4B, with a range spanning $250M to $32.0B. These methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $2.0B (floor) to $4.6B (ceiling), with a median of $3.2B.

The current $2.0B valuation aligns with the peer-median result and represents the lower bound of the derived range. Key uncertainties include the substantial dispersion within the peer set—driven by heterogeneous stage and technology maturity—and the limited public comparables available for traditional valuation anchoring in this nascent sector.
